personal.name: Moein Tash
personal.title: PhD in Artificial Intelligence (NLP)
personal.tagline: I am passionate about Natural Language Processing, Deep Learning and building intelligent solutions that make a real impact.

chatbotKnowledge.answer: Moein Tash's research area is Natural Language Processing (NLP). His work focuses on deep learning, transformers, sentiment analysis, hate/hope speech detection, social support detection, and FinTech NLP.

Moein Tash is a PhD researcher in Artificial Intelligence (NLP) at IPN Mexico City and currently a Lecturer at Universidad Panamericana, CDMX. He specializes in Natural Language Processing and Social Media Mining.

Moein is completing his PhD in Computer Science (NLP) at Instituto Politécnico Nacional (IPN), Mexico City (2022-2026), at the Gelbukh Lab. He holds an M.Sc. with Distinction and a B.Sc. in Computer Science from the University of Sistan and Baluchestan, Iran.

Moein has 12 journal papers (8 published in Q1/Q2 journals like Scientific Reports, Cognitive Computation, Heliyon, and PLOS ONE, plus 4 under review) and 11 conference papers in venues including ACL workshops, IberLEF, ICON, and DravidianLangTech.

Moein currently teaches NLP at Universidad Panamericana (since July 2025) and researches at IPN's Gelbukh Lab. Previously, he was a Machine Learning Engineer at Lidoma (2019-2022) and a Python Developer at Hi-Web (2017-2018).
You can reach Moein at moein.tash@gmail.com. He is also active on LinkedIn, GitHub, and Google Scholar — always open to academic collaboration and research discussions.
Moein is fluent in English, native in Persian and Baluchi, and has intermediate Spanish.

Moein's tech stack: Python, TensorFlow, Keras, Hugging Face, Scikit-learn, Pandas, NumPy, Streamlit, LangChain, FAISS, and Azure ML. NLP expertise includes hate/hope speech detection, sentiment analysis, NER, code-mixed text handling, and language identification.

Some notable projects: a URL-based RAG chatbot (Streamlit + LangChain + FAISS), a bilingual social support dataset (English & Spanish), a crypto emotion analysis system using SenticNet, and the IberLEF 2026 SSD Shared Task he organizes.

Moein holds the SECIHTI Scholarship from the Government of Mexico, graduated his M.Sc. with Distinction, is the Main Organizer of the SSD Shared Task at IberLEF 2026, a Program Committee Member at SemEval-2027, and has reviewed 10+ articles for international conferences.
Moein is based in Mexico City, Mexico, where he is pursuing his PhD at IPN and teaching at Universidad Panamericana.

Moein's NLP research covers social support detection, hate speech detection, hope speech detection, sentiment analysis, language identification, and FinTech NLP — particularly cryptocurrency discourse analysis using SenticNet and psycholinguistics.
Moein has been a Lecturer at Universidad Panamericana, CDMX since July 2025, where he teaches graduate-level courses in Natural Language Processing (NLP).

greetings: Hello! I'm Moein's AI assistant. Feel free to ask me anything about Moein Tash.
offTopicResponse: I can only answer questions related to Moein Tash. Please ask something about his background, experience, skills, or research.
